title: Ordered Hierarchical Target Statistics for XGB
summary: Current XGB side mostly uses category codes/frequencies, while CatBoost literature shows ordered target statistics and ordered boosting reduce prediction shift for high-cardinality categoricals.
rationale: Current XGB side mostly uses category codes/frequencies, while CatBoost literature shows ordered target statistics and ordered boosting reduce prediction shift for high-cardinality categoricals. This dataset has strong Driver/Race/Compound/Stint structure that simple codes likely underuse.
implementation_hint: Create fold-safe ordered empirical-Bayes encodings with nested fallbacks such as Driver, Driver x Compound, Race x Compound, Driver x Stint, and Year x Race x Compound. Within each training fold, compute them in race-time order or via multiple random permutations, then feed them to XGB/linear heads.
expected_effect: Should strengthen the non-CatBoost branch, especially on sparse drivers/compounds, and reduce variance from brittle category coding.
risk: Easy to leak validation targets if encodings are not strictly OOF; overly deep combinations may overfit because some keys are very sparse.
